name: Chicken Asparagus & Red Pepper
recipeIngredient: 2 medium Garlic cloves, peeled and minced
1 tsp Cornstarch blended with 1/4 cup Chicken broth
recipeInstructions:
1. Pound the chicken breasts as you would for scaloppine, then slice them crosswise at 1/2-inch intervals.
2. Marinate the chicken for 30 minutes at room temperature in a mixture of ginger, garlic, soy sauce, port (or Madeira), and 1 tablespoon of sesame oil.
3. Trim the coarse stem ends from the asparagus. Peel the asparagus and slice them crosswise, slightly on the diagonal, into 1-inch lengths.
4. Peel and halve the onion, then cut each half into thin slices.
5. Core, seed, and slice the red bell pepper lengthwise into 1/4-inch strips.
6. Set a wok or large skillet over high heat. Add the chili oil and 1 tablespoon of remaining sesame oil and heat the oil for about 1 minute until ripples appear on the wok bottom.
7. Add the onion and red pepper and stir-fry the mixture for 1 minute. Cover the wok and allow the mixture to steam for 1 minute.
8. Using a slotted spoon, remove the vegetables from the wok to a heatproof plate.
9. Add the remaining sesame oil to the wok, pour in the chicken and marinade, and stir-fry over high heat for 2 minutes.
10. Blend in the cornstarch mixture, stirring it for about 1 minute until the sauce has thickened and becomes clear.
11. Return the vegetables to the wok, toss the mixture lightly for about 30 seconds, and serve. Accompany the chicken with fluffy boiled rice.
